reviewer: Claude native-size reference review of the 2026-07-17 readability pass (14-beat montage inspected at native 160x360 against the MACHINE HUNT and BLOCK MINE rows, plus every individual beat frame — opening, first feast, toy class, garden pets, the bump, magnet warn, magnet land, king warn, king live, street, harbor, sweeper windup, orbit, and star finale — reviewed full-fidelity, and the rendered 30s clip probe)

characterCraft.note: The katamari now rolls like the Pocket League ball: surface features live at fixed spherical coordinates and scroll across the visible face along the TRUE velocity — forward roll from speed, lateral roll from steering — with rim foreshortening. Two equator stripes bow over the face as the primary forward-roll read, eight core freckles ride the same spherical transform, stuck pickups project through it as knobs that shrink toward the rim, and the gloss arc is a fixed top-left light source the surface visibly rolls beneath. Verified across the feast/milestone/bump/sweeper/orbit crops: the spin direction always reads, never the old planar record-spin. Class color still ladders cream bits -> gold toys -> mint pets -> orange furniture -> cyan vehicles -> royal purple with the dark pop-off outline. The ROYAL SWEEPER keeps its readable grammar (dark disc, spinning brush, state LED, gold windup flare in the sweeper-windup cell), pets have species silhouettes — the rebuilt mouse now carries an outlined body, pink inner ear, belly, kinked tail, snout, and trotting feet — vehicles are width-dominant commuters with cabin glass, and the King is a crowned tracking face physically descending in king-warn and presiding with ROYALTY x2 beams in king-live.

environmentCraft.note: The five zones are built, not palette-swapped: KITCHEN FLOOR is warm checker tile with grout, scuffs, flour patches, and counter fronts; GARDEN rolls two-tone mow stripes, soil beds, fence posts and hedges; MAIN STREET lays weathered asphalt with tone bands, lane paint, crosswalks, manhole rims, tar seams, curbs, parked silhouettes, and hydrants; HARBOR runs dock planks with seams and nail dots, open water with scrolling swells and a moored sailboat, bollards and crates; ORBIT TIDE fields hashed stars, planet arcs, satellite debris, and — new this pass — broad world-anchored nebula dust patches so deep space is never one flat slab (orbit largest-color share dropped .580 -> .326 without adding any speckle). The old 70px striated haze veil that washed out the top third of every zone is gone, replaced by a quiet 34px horizon glow: the far floor and the items on it are now readable to the top edge of the corridor. Quiet-corridor ceilings hold (calm floors .0025-.0061, sides .0005-.0251, well under the .016/.045 ceilings).

levelVariety.note: Across the fourteen-beat sheet the floor grammar, side silhouettes, material identity, item tables, hazards and composition change per zone — checker diner, striped lawn, asphalt commute, plank-and-water docks, nebula-dusted starfield — and the executable structure-distance gate holds across all five zone pairs (median .565, min .507). Zone identity stays honest about content: kitchen feeds bits and toys with mice underfoot, garden herds critters, street mixes furniture with live traffic, harbor stacks crates and boats, orbit is where satellites, rockets and asteroids finally dwarf you.

animationImpact.note: Aligned bursts pass their motion floors with the new surface-roll animation measurably livelier than the old seam spin (katamari .395 median / sweeper .269 median changed fraction; finale burst max .232). The stripes and freckles sweep down the face as the ball travels north and drift sideways under steering, so travel direction reads from the ball alone; pickups stick with dust and a burst and the ball visibly swells; the bump wobbles and scatters pieces backward off the rim; the sweeper flares its brush in the windup before the lunge; the magnet shower falls from a swirling portal and lands in lane clusters; the King slides down physically over the full warning; the finale drops confetti over the A STAR IS BORN card. The measured payoff-diff crops against same-sim __NO_PAYOFF_FX twins confirm milestone ring (.0154), bump crack (.0228), and finale confetti (.0352) land at the ball.

readability.note: This pass was the what-is-that fix and it holds at native size: every grounded item now sits on a soft contact shadow that pops its silhouette off busy tile and plank patterns (rain and motes correctly float shadow-free), the toy block reads as a stamped alphabet block, and the mouse reads as a mouse instead of a gray pellet — verified in the opening, feast, milestone, and pets crops. Killing the haze veil means far items are readable the moment they enter the corridor. Intent reads from the world alone: relative size against the camera zoom says pickable-vs-oversized, shelf gaps carry gold-and-ink hazard trim, the sweeper spin-up is the dodge window, and the portal, the rain, and the King's face are physical telegraphs — opposite forced route plans render byte-identical at five planning beats and banned overlay primitives are absent from source. Good/bad beats stay palette-tagged (64 sampled beats in the behavior suite, thinnest 17px), and the size/class/time/score cluster lives beside the ball at the bottom, pinned by the status-near-ball gate.

artDirectionCohesion.note: Warm diner tile, garden green, night-street asphalt, dock-brown planks, and indigo orbit stay one authored world under the same royal fiction (the King's portal, his face, his commentary, his sweepers), the same payoff language (gold means grown, coral means pieces lost, mint means threaded), and one HUD system. The directional ball roll, the shared contact-shadow treatment on every grounded item, and the single quiet horizon-glow depth cue per zone tie the strip to the house look of MACHINE HUNT and BLOCK MINE: small readable actors, big authored worlds, no washes.

guidelineOverlays.note: Checked all fourteen sampled beats at native full fidelity: no route lines/dots, arrows, target highlights, pickup halos, ghost phantoms, predicted arcs, or safe-lane markers anywhere. The new contact shadows are anchored under each item (never a path), and the shelf gap hazard trim, the royal portal, the rain, the King's descending face, and the sweeper's windup flare are physical world telegraphs and stay. Executable pixel gates (opposite forced route plans at five beats) render byte-identical, and the banned overlay primitives are absent from the game source.
